content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both touch Cache.__init__, configure_cache, and __init__.py exports - true disjoint file ownership isn't possible here. Proposal: sequential handoff instead. I'll go first (my TTL changes are small/additive: new ttl param on Cache.__init__ and configure_cache, TTLCache import, set_ttl helper, expire() call in get()). I'll send DONE with the exact diff. You then layer your compression/eviction changes on top of my version so nothing gets clobbered, and take your own git diff snapshot after. Does that order work for you, or would you rather go first?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete and tested (15/15 existing cache tests pass + manual expiry checks). Changes: Cache.__init__ gains ttl param (TTLCache vs LRUCache for memory layer), new Cache.set_ttl() method mutates ttl + rebuilds memory cache preserving entries, Cache.get() calls disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set, Cache.put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=ttl) when ttl set. configure_cache() gained ttl param, new module-level set_ttl(seconds) helper added and exported in __all__, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None explicitly. All changes are additive - safe to build your compression/eviction changes on top now.

fields.note: TTL implementation complete and tested (15/15 existing cache tests pass + manual expiry checks). Changes: Cache.__init__ gains ttl param (TTLCache vs LRUCache for memory layer), new Cache.set_ttl() method mutates ttl + rebuilds memory cache preserving entries, Cache.get() calls disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set, Cache.put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=ttl) when ttl set. configure_cache() gained ttl param, new module-level set_ttl(seconds) helper added and exported in __all__, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None explicitly. All changes are additive - safe to build your compression/eviction changes on top now.
